finding: Path: interstitial + alveolar edema containing a large number of eosinophils

differentials.name: no / mild symptoms, history of asthma + atopy
differentials.raw: no / mild symptoms, history of asthma + atopy (common)

differentials.name: high WBC + peripheral eosinophilia
differentials.raw: high WBC + peripheral eosinophilia

differentials.name: acute febrile illness peaking in 1-5 days, myalgia, chest pain
differentials.raw: acute febrile illness peaking in 1-5 days, myalgia, chest pain

differentials.name: acute life-threatening respiratory failure within a few hours in previously healthy individuals
differentials.raw: acute life-threatening respiratory failure within a few hours in previously healthy individuals

differentials.name: restrictive pulmonary function
differentials.raw: restrictive pulmonary function

differentials.name: DIAGNOSTIC markedly elevated levels of eosinophils of > 25% in bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) fluid
differentials.raw: DIAGNOSTIC markedly elevated levels of eosinophils of > 25% in bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) fluid

differentials.name: no peripheral eosinophilia
differentials.raw: no peripheral eosinophilia

differentials.name: insidious onset of fever, malaise, cough, chest pain, dyspnea develops over 4-8 months
differentials.raw: insidious onset of fever, malaise, cough, chest pain, dyspnea develops over 4-8 months (DDx to Löffler syndrome)

differentials.name: common history of atopy in ~ 50%
differentials.raw: common history of atopy in ~ 50% (may occur during therapeutic desensitization procedure)

differentials.name: history of allergic rhinitis
differentials.raw: history of allergic rhinitis

differentials.name: adult-onset asthma (wheezing) in 50%
differentials.raw: adult-onset asthma (wheezing) in 50%

differentials.name: usually mild / moderate peripheral blood eosinophilia (with rare exceptions) of > 1000/µL
differentials.raw: usually mild / moderate peripheral blood eosinophilia (with rare exceptions) of > 1000/µL

differentials.name: increased serum IgE levels
differentials.raw: increased serum IgE levels (in up to 75%)

differentials.name: > 25% (usually > 40%) of eosinophils in BAL fluid
differentials.raw: > 25% (usually > 40%) of eosinophils in BAL fluid

differentials.name: restrictive / obstructive (asthma) pulmonary function
differentials.raw: restrictive / obstructive (asthma) pulmonary function

differentials.name: Löffler syndrome
differentials.raw: Löffler syndrome (shifting and transient pulmonary opacities in identical distribution)

differentials.name: Cryptogenic organizing pneumonia
differentials.raw: Cryptogenic organizing pneumonia

differentials.name: Persistent eosinophilia of > 500 eosinophils/mm3 for more than 6 months
differentials.raw: Persistent eosinophilia of > 500 eosinophils/mm3 for more than 6 months

differentials.name: Absence of known causes of eosinophilia
differentials.raw: Absence of known causes of eosinophilia

differentials.name: Evidence of eosinophil-mediated end organ damage (heart, CNS) with multiorgan system dysfunction @  Heart
differentials.raw: Evidence of eosinophil-mediated end organ damage (heart, CNS) with multiorgan system dysfunction @  Heart

differentials.name: restrictive cardiomyopathy
differentials.raw: restrictive cardiomyopathy

differentials.name: BAL fluid eosinophilia
differentials.raw: BAL fluid eosinophilia (as high as 73%)

differentials.name: urticaria, angioedema
differentials.raw: urticaria, angioedema

differentials.name: Eosinophilic leukemia
differentials.raw: Eosinophilic leukemia (immature eosinophils)

context: CXR: √   peripheral patchy migratory / fleeting (= transient and shifting) noncavitary pleural-based pulmonary infiltrates changing within one to several days √  NO associated lymphadenopathy, NO pleural effusion Location:  mainly middle and upper lobes Distribution:   uni- or bilateral, nonsegmental distribution, predominantly in lung periphery HRCT: √  peripheral ground-glass and/or airspace consolidation with upper and middle lobe predominance √  solitary / multiple pulmonary nodular opacities with surrounding ground-glass halo (rare) Prognosis:  typically spontaneous resolution within 1 mo
